channel.title: Maria Evangelinou — Bioinformatician
channel.description: Recent content on Maria Evangelinou — Bioinformatician

item.title: Legal notice
item.description:
Provider

This website is a personal, non-commercial portfolio operated by:

  • Maria Evangelinou — Bioinformatician
  • Crete, Greece
  • Email: evangelinoumar@gmail.com
  • Phone: (+30)698-409-5908

Maria Evangelinou is responsible for the content of this website.

Purpose

The site presents professional and academic information (a curriculum vitae, projects and publications). It is provided for information purposes and does not constitute an offer, advice or a binding commitment.

Liability for content

Content has been prepared with care, but no guarantee is given as to its accuracy, completeness or timeliness. As a private individual, the provider is responsible for own content under the general laws of Greece (European Union).

item.title: Privacy policy
item.description:
This policy explains how personal data is handled when you visit this website. It is written to align with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR, Regulation (EU) 2016/679) and Greek data-protection law.

Data controller

The controller responsible for this website is:

  • Maria Evangelinou
  • Crete, Greece
  • Email: evangelinoumar@gmail.com

The short version

This is a static personal website. It contains no contact forms, no analytics, no advertising and no tracking technologies. It does not set cookies, and it does not collect or store personal data about you on any server controlled by me.

item.title: About
item.description:
I started in applied mathematics and moved toward biology because that is where some of the most interesting modelling problems live. Today I work at the intersection of the two: using statistics and code to make sense of genetic data.

My master’s research benchmarks kinship software under extreme demographic scenarios — stress-testing the tools other researchers rely on, so their conclusions hold up. Earlier, my undergraduate thesis tested admixture-inference software across diverse demographic models. Both share a theme I care about: knowing not just what a method outputs, but when it can be trusted.

item.title: Contact
item.description: Whether it’s a role in genomics or population genetics, a pharmaceutical-research problem, or a project where mathematics and biology meet — I’d be glad to hear from you. Reach me directly through any of the channels below.
